target.title: docs: add direct Kubernetes manifest apply path
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #91455.
- Removes the Helm-focused wording from the Kubernetes install guide.
- Adds `scripts/k8s/manifest.yaml` as a single-file manifest for direct `kubectl apply` workflows.
- Keeps the existing `scripts/k8s/manifests/` and `deploy.sh` path unchanged.

## Verification

- `pnpm check:docs`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base upstream/main --head HEAD --timed -- docs/install/kubernetes.md scripts/k8s/manifest.yaml`
- Verified `scripts/k8s/manifest.yaml` semantically matches `kubectl kustomize scripts/k8s/manifests`.
- Verified in a temporary Kind cluster: Secret created, `scripts/k8s/manifest.yaml` applied, Deployment reached `1/1`, PVC bound, `/healthz` live, `/readyz` ready.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Kubernetes docs no longer frame Helm negatively and now provide a direct single-file manifest path for `kubectl apply`.

Real environment tested: Local Kind Kubernetes cluster using Docker.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary Kind cluster, created the `openclaw` namespace and `openclaw-secrets`, applied `scripts/k8s/manifest.yaml`, waited for rollout, then port-forwarded `svc/openclaw`.

Evidence after fix: `deployment/openclaw` reached `1/1`, pod was `Running`, PVC was `Bound`, and the service answered health probes.

Observed result after fix: `/healthz` returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}` and `/readyz` returned `{"ready":true,...}`.

What was not tested: Live model-provider calls; the Kind smoke used a dummy provider key and only verified Kubernetes deployment, gateway startup, and health/readiness.
